entry.title: S4HANA Public Cloud - CBO Upsert Error 'Expected type is 'Edm.Boolean''
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm trying an upsert to a custom CBO but the expected types are sort of strange.

 

It does not accept the values a GET request response.
Also numeric is not accepted as '1.00' but 1.00m

Example for the error:

https://xxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_API_TEST_CDS/YY1_API_TESTSap_upsert?NumericID='0000000001'&SAP_Description='API-Test'&Amount_V=2.00m&Amount_C='Amount_C'&TextToChange='AV'&Quantity_V=9.99m&Quantity_U='G'&CheckBox=true

CheckBox = true is not considered as boolean, nor is 'true' or 'X' or 1 or whatever.

Has anyone solved this?

entry.title: Re: SAP Replication flow from SAP Datasphere to S3
entry.content:
For a replication flow to use initial and delta you need to enable delta capture on your local table.

You will need to create a new local table and set the toggle "Delta Capture" ON in the general table properties.

Error - Annotation 'Hierarchy.parentChild' must be an array type

@analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true

and $projection.ValidityEndDate = _Hierarchy.ValidityEndDate //Reviewed

entry.title: Re: DCD1 condition type is getting determined for US comp...
entry.content:
Hello,

The issue of the DCD1 (Cash Discount) condition type not getting determined for US company codes could be due to several reasons related to the payment terms configuration and master data setup. Here are some possible causes and solutions:

1. **Payment Terms Configuration**:
- Ensure that the payment terms associated with the preliminary billing document are correctly configured in the system. The CDS views for payment terms provide details about cash discount conditions and net due days. Check if the payment terms description and conditions align with the expected cash discount.

2. **Customer Master Data**:
- Verify that the payment terms in the customer master data are correctly maintained. Any discrepancy in the payment terms can lead to issues with the determination of the cash discount condition type.

3. **Condition Record**:
- Check if there is a valid condition record for the DCD1 condition type for the relevant combination of parameters (e.g., payer, document category, sales organization) specified in the billing document. The absence of a valid condition record could prevent the cash discount from being applied.

4. **Pricing Procedure**:
- Ensure that the pricing procedure used in the billing document includes the DCD1 condition type and that it is correctly configured to be determined based on the payment terms.

5. **Company Code Specific Settings**:
- Verify any specific settings or overrides for US company codes that might affect the determination of payment terms or cash discounts. Company code-specific configurations can sometimes alter standard behavior.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Issue with Hierarchy CDS View
entry.content:
The error "Annotation 'Hierarchy.parentChild' must be an array type" occurs because the annotation @Hierarchy.parentChild expects an array format,
but it may have been declared incorrectly in your CDS view.

Correct Syntax for a Hierarchy CDS View
Make sure that your CDS view follows the correct syntax for defining a hierarchy. Below is the correct format:
----------abap
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_HIERARCHY'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS View with Hierarchy Node'
@Hierarchy.root: 'ROOT_NODE'
@Hierarchy.parentChild: [{ parent: 'PARENT_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }]
@Hierarchy.level: 'HIER_LEVEL'
@Hierarchy.ancestors: [{ ancestor: 'ANCESTOR_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }]
define view ZCDS_HIERARCHY as select from <your_table>
{
key node_id as CHILD_NODE,
parent_id as PARENT_NODE,
hierarchy_level as HIER_LEVEL,
ancestor_id as ANCESTOR_NODE,
node_name
}

Key Fixes:
1. @Hierarchy.parentChild must be an array
- Use `[{ parent: 'PARENT_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }]`
- Incorrect: `@Hierarchy.parentChild: { parent: 'PARENT_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }` (missing array brackets `[]`)

2. Ensure Parent-Child Fields Exist
- `PARENT_NODE` and `CHILD_NODE` should be actual fields in your table.

3. Optional Annotations:
- Use `@Hierarchy.root` for defining the root node.
- `@Hierarchy.ancestors` can be used if needed.

Next Steps:
- Try correcting your CDS view based on the syntax above.
- If the issue persists, share your full CDS view so I can check it for further errors.

entry.title: Re: Automatic OP clearing when using an alternative payer
entry.content:
In SAP Public Cloud (FI-only), the Alternative Payer field in the Business Partner does not automatically trigger clearing when using payment advice.
To enable automatic clearing:
1. Define a Clearing Rule in Transaction OB74 to allow open item clearing between the customer and alternative payer.
2. Use a Customer Clearing Account (via F-30 or F-32) to link payments to the correct open items.
3. Enable BAdI Implementation (e.g., FI_PAYMEDIUM_SAMPLE_41) to enhance payment processing logic.
4. Ensure Alternative Payer is Assigned in the Payment Advice and Document Posting.
5. Check Automatic Clearing Configurations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud SSCUI settings.

entry.title: Re: You cannot change the UoM group unless the new UoM gr...
entry.content:
Hi @Underdog ,

SAP Business One requires that any new UoM group assigned to an item must include all the conversion rules present in the original UoM group. This ensures consistency in how quantities are calculated and avoids discrepancies in historical data. If the new UoM group does not replicate or extend the existing conversion rules, the system will block the change.

Looks like this is being applied even when the item is a brand new one, as you described. 

Additionally, have you attempted to replicate this scenario with another item, whether new or old?

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Upgrading to Latest Goal Management – Cycle Planning & Preparedness – Best Practice Approach
entry.content:
@Tbrown 

  • Navigate to Report center
  • Click “New”
  • Click “Table”
  • Click Single Domain
  • Select “Goal Management” as the data you wish to start with
  • Define Team: Select any Team Manager and select “Other Filters” for the Team Reporting Type
  • Click on Data Sets and choose the Goal Template that needs to be converted, which contains the unsupported table fields
  • Click on “Columns” and select all columns configured for the Target and Tasks table
  • Click the Objective plan ID and Objective ID

Then through Import Performance Goals tool, in your csv import file, use ACTION: UPDATE to edit the goals' status value. Here's the help guide on how to import: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PERFORMANCE_AND_GOALS/4a24e48b5c384b18a81f6b661305021e/7f279b4b774e41e1bb69594021577494.html

entry.content:

  • ....

 

This is already and an advanced method.  Lets step back to the fundamental idea.

YEOMAN is a wide use code generator that helps you to generate files or changes content of files within your project. Fundamentals are explained on the YEOMAN page.

In SAP BUILD you can use YEOMAN in a terminal and type "yo":

entry.title: Re: Mastering BDC Table Control: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content: Note that the term BDC used for "Batch Data Collection", has been obsolete for a long time and was renamed "Batch Input". You won't find BDC in the glossary of the ABAP Documentation, you will find Batch Input. ABAP Keyword Documentation

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
You are right, that the print is generally for the warehouse order but since the warehouse tasks are a child of the warehouse order, the printing is also triggered during warehouse task confirmation.

If you set up the OPD to print for warehouse order status "D", then printing should be triggered for the confirmation WT 1-49, if you additionally set up to print for status "C", then printing should also be triggered for the confirmation of WT 50.

For the confirmation of WT 1-49, the form data provider will pass all open warehouse task. So for example, when confirming WT 1, the open warehouse tasks 2-50 will be passed to the form. To my understanding when confirming WT 1 you want to print for WT 1 - which is exactly the WT which is not passed to the form. To achieve this you can implement BAdI  /SCWM/EX4_OM_WHO_EXTEND_WT  to read the confirmed warehouse tasks for that warehouse order from CDS view I_EWM_WarehouseTask_2. For WT 1 the logic would be easy, because there is then only one confirmed WT. To make sure the logic also works for WT 2-50, it is probably easiest to sort the confirmed warehouse task descending by ConfirmationUTCDateTime.

I hope this helps.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: E-Kanban(PK13N/PK21) set Kanban Container on full with batch number
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP kanban with replenishment strategy for external procurement and batch managed components is currently not possible.

Error message is displayed, when the kanban status was set to full that a batch number must be insert for the batch managed component. There’s no possibility to select one entry or enter the batch number at the kanban board.

Kanban control cycle is created for material XXXX. Replenishment strategy external procurement 0007- Summarized JIT calls (w/o summary). Scheduling agreement is also defined for the vendor in the sap system.

Bill of material for different components (batch / none batch managed) is defined and released in plant XXX.

Stock of this materials is provided to the vendor and can be displayed with transaction ME2O.

With the goods receipt posting (set status green in the kanban board) of the produced material, the stock of the provided components needs to be reduced.

Goods movement of the material with movement type 543 will be done automatically with the goods receipt posting for none batch managed components, but for the other ones an error message is displayed

PK13N:

Material:3918792B not batch number

When you click the container and set to full->

Error Message:

CS03

Material: 3918792B

Material 39188019 needs a batch number.

entry.title: Re: qRFC inbound IDOC error not lock the queue
entry.content:
We found an enhacement spot that solved that: IDOC_INPUT_QUEUE_CONTINUE. 

On method INPUT_QUEUE_CONTINUE~QUEUE_CONTINUE you can control if the queue should continue or not with parameter RP_QUEUE_CONTINUE ('X' - queue should continue processing)

To know wich queue are you managing you have parameters IDOC_CONTROL (Control Record (IDoc)) and IDOC_QRFC_QUEUENAME (Name of tRFC Queue).

I hope it helps someone. Best regards.

entry.title: SAP 10 FP 2405 Server Resources
entry.content:
Experts,

Having upgraded several clients to SAP 10 FP 2405 MS SQL version we have noticed the resources maxing up and quite a bit of bloatware with this version. Both RAM and CPU are frequently maxing out. The server is slow and constantly freezes.

For example 16 GB RAM on a 4 Core server is maxing out frequently with not more than 3 users connected at a time. This gets worse with service layer activated. How else can we use the web client ?

I also cannot explain the extra services running - SAPMachine Binary, Apache Commons, Apache HTTP server - running and seemingly taking up resources. What is the optimal server spec required for running this version ? Screenshot attached with two users logged in. RAM is at 81% . RAM capacity is 16GB.
